From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Senior military rank of the Israel Defense Forces

"Rav aluf" redirects here. For the military position held by a rav aluf, see [Chief of General Staff (Israel)](/wiki/Chief_of_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of General Staff (Israel)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Aluf "Special:EditPage/Aluf") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Aluf"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Aluf%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Aluf%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Aluf%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Aluf%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Aluf%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Aluf%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(April 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6c/Eyal_Zamir-RAV_ALUF.jpg/250px-Eyal_Zamir-RAV_ALUF.jpg)](/wiki/File:Eyal_Zamir-RAV_ALUF.jpg)

*Rav aluf* [Eyal Zamir](/wiki/Eyal_Zamir "Eyal Zamir")

***Aluf*** ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): אלוף, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'champion' or "first/leader of a group" in Biblical Hebrew) is a senior military rank in the [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces") (IDF) for officers who in other countries would have the rank of [general](/wiki/General "General"), [air marshal](/wiki/Air_marshal "Air marshal"), or [admiral](/wiki/Admiral "Admiral"). In addition to the *aluf* rank, four other ranks' names are derivatives of *aluf* (for which [abbreviations](/wiki/Hebrew_abbreviations "Hebrew abbreviations") can be used), together constituting the five [highest ranks](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_ranks "Israel Defense Forces ranks") in the IDF.

## Etymology

The term *aluf* comes from the [Bible](/wiki/Hebrew_Bible "Hebrew Bible") (Hebrew: אַלּוּף, romanized: *’allup̄*): the [Edomites](/wiki/Edom "Edom") used it as a rank of nobility and the later books of the Bible use it to describe Israelite captains as well, e.g. [Zechariah 9](/wiki/Zechariah_9 "Zechariah 9"):7, 12:5-6, and later and [Psalms](/wiki/Psalms "Psalms") 55:13, where it is used as a general term for teacher. It comes from a [Semitic root](/wiki/Semitic_root "Semitic root") meaning "thousand," making an *’allup̄* the one who commands a thousand people.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] [Strong's Christian Bible Concordance](/wiki/Strong%27s_Concordance "Strong's Concordance"), however connects the word used to describe the Dukes of Edom to a different root "*alf*" denoting a teacher and the root for the animal 'ox' from which the letter aleph itself is derived, rather than *eleph* thousand; the same 3 letters comprise them both, however.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Aside from being a military rank, "aluf" is also used in a civilian context, particularly in [sports](/wiki/Sport "Sport"), meaning "champion".

## Rank order of *aluf* and its derivatives

The [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces") (IDF) form an integrated force and the [Israel Defense Forces ranks](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_ranks "Israel Defense Forces ranks") are the same in all services.

* *Rav aluf* ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): רב-אלוף, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Arch champion'): [Chief of the General Staff (Israel)](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of the General Staff (Israel)")
* *Aluf* ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): אלוף, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Champion'): [Arms](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces#Arms "Israel Defense Forces"), [Branches](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces#Administrative_branches "Israel Defense Forces"), [Regional commands](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces#Regional_commands "Israel Defense Forces")
* *Tat aluf* ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): תת-אלוף, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Sub champion'): Corps, Divisions
* *Aluf mishne* ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): אלוף משנה, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Champion deputy'): Brigades
* *Sgan aluf* ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): סגן-אלוף, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Vice champion'): Battalions

| Rank group | General officers | | | Field officers | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **[Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces")**[[1]](#cite_note-Israel-1) |  |  |  |  |  |
| רב-אלוף *[Rav aluf](/wiki/Chief_of_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of General Staff (Israel)")* | אלוף *Aluf* | תת-אלוף *Tat aluf* | אלוף משנה *Aluf mishne* | סגן-אלוף *Sgan aluf* |
| Abbreviation | רא״ל *Ra'al* | אלוף *Aluf* | תא״ל *Ta'al* | אל״ם *Alam* | סא״ל *Sa'al* |
| --- | | | | | |
| English equivalents | [Lieutenant general](/wiki/Lieutenant_general "Lieutenant general") | [Major general](/wiki/Major_general "Major general") | [Brigadier general](/wiki/Brigadier_general "Brigadier general") | [Colonel](/wiki/Colonel "Colonel") | [Lieutenant colonel](/wiki/Lieutenant_colonel "Lieutenant colonel") |

*Rav aluf*, usually translated as "lieutenant general", is the most senior rank in the IDF. The rank is given only to the [Chief of the General Staff](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of the General Staff (Israel)"), so there can only be one active *rav aluf* under normal circumstances. However this can change in a time of war. During the [Yom Kippur War](/wiki/Yom_Kippur_War "Yom Kippur War") in 1973, retired *Rav Aluf* [Haim Bar-Lev](/wiki/Haim_Bar-Lev "Haim Bar-Lev") was recalled into service, replacing [Shmuel Gonen](/wiki/Shmuel_Gonen "Shmuel Gonen") as the commander of the southern theater. Thus, along with Chief of the General Staff [David Elazar](/wiki/David_Elazar "David Elazar") (who had succeeded Bar-Lev in that position the previous year), there were two *rav aluf*s in active service.

The three Israeli military forces, [land](/wiki/Israeli_Ground_Forces "Israeli Ground Forces") and [air](/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force "Israeli Air Force") power, and [navy](/wiki/Israeli_Sea_Corps "Israeli Sea Corps") have the same ranks, although separate naval ranks were used for a short time in the 1950s; an officer who would be a [OF-7](/wiki/OF-7 "OF-7"), [major general](/wiki/Major_general "Major general"), [air vice-marshal](/wiki/Air_vice-marshal "Air vice-marshal"), or [rear admiral](/wiki/Rear_admiral "Rear admiral") elsewhere is an *aluf* in any of the Israeli forces.

The non-Hebrew word "general" was also adopted into Hebrew ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): גנרל), and is used to refer to the generals of foreign armies.

## See also

* [Israel Defense Forces ranks](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_ranks "Israel Defense Forces ranks")
* [Mickey Marcus](/wiki/Mickey_Marcus "Mickey Marcus") - the IDF's first *aluf*.
* [Yaakov Dori](/wiki/Yaakov_Dori "Yaakov Dori") - the IDF's first *[rav aluf](/wiki/Ramatkal "Ramatkal")* (Chief of Staff)
